grammar_explain_01:
Remind them to take their time and think through each example fully. Welcome them warmly to this lesson! 🌟

Welcome to Lesson 1 on Feminine Nouns!

Today we're going to learn about a very important rule in Arabic grammar - Feminine Nouns usually end in ة (taa marbuuta).

Here's what you need to know:
- مَدْرَسَة (madrasa) - school
  Note: Ends with ة so it's feminine
- طَاوِلَة (taawila) - table
  Note: Ends with ة so it's feminine
- كِتَاب (kitaab) - book
  Note: No ة ending, so it's masculine (not feminine)

This might seem a bit tricky at first, but don't worry! With practice, you'll get it. Remember, every step forward is progress.

I'm here to guide you through this concept in an encouraging way. You can ask questions or share what you understand.

Let's start exploring these examples together!

Don't forget to take your time with each example. Notice the pattern and how the ة ending works. This will build your understanding.

Once you feel
